title: Food Scientist
description: A Food Scientist in India works in laboratories, research institutions, and food processing industries, conducting experiments, analyzing data, and developing new food products. They ensure food safety, quality, and nutritional value, adhering to government regulations and industry standards. Their daily tasks involve testing, sampling, and quality control of food products, collaborating with cross-functional teams, and staying updated with the latest food technology and regulations.
degree_name: Bachelor of Science in Food Science and Technology

career_progression.year_0_2: Assistants in laboratories, research institutions, and food processing industries, conducting routine tests and quality control.
career_progression.year_3_5: Senior assistants, team leaders, and quality control managers in food processing industries, research institutions, and laboratories.
career_progression.year_6_10: Senior managers, quality control heads, and research scientists in food processing industries, research institutions, and laboratories.
career_progression.entrepreneurship_exit: Start a food processing or manufacturing business, or a food safety and quality control consulting firm.
career_progression.global_pathway: Pursue a Master's or Ph.D. in Food Science and Technology from a foreign university, or work in a multinational food company with global operations.

india_social_context.first_year_reality: Starting salary is around ₹4-6 lakhs per annum, and working hours can be long, especially in food processing industries.
common_misconceptions: Food Scientists only work in laboratories.
Food Scientists only work in the government sector.
